notable_features: Plan model with 5-tier subscription system (Free, Starter, Professional, Business, Enterprise)
Flexible JSON fields for features and limits storage
SubscriptionStatus and InvoiceStatus enums for state management
Trial period tracking with trialStart and trialEnd
Payment method tracking and invoice lifecycle management
Strategic indexes on frequently queried fields (status, planId, currentPeriodEnd, dueDate)

file_insights.purpose: Merge multiple Prisma schema files into single schema.prisma
key_functions: generateHeader() - Creates auto-generated file header with timestamp
generateSeparator() - Creates section separators between schema files
readSchemaFile() - Reads and trims Prisma schema content

patterns_discovered.description: Schema split across multiple .prisma files (base, core, subscription) with merge script
patterns_discovered.benefit: Improved maintainability, clear separation of concerns by domain

patterns_discovered.description: Critical files (base with datasource/generator) merged first, then domain modules, then others alphabetically
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures datasource and generator definitions appear before entity definitions

patterns_discovered.description: Use of enums (SubscriptionStatus, InvoiceStatus, PaymentMethod) for constrained state
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type safety and limited valid states at database level

patterns_discovered.description: Complex, variable data stored in JSON fields (features, limits, lineItems, metadata)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Schema flexibility without creating new normalized tables for every possible attribute

patterns_discovered.description: Indexes on foreign keys, status fields, and date range queries
patterns_discovered.benefit: Optimized query performance for common billing/subscription operations

patterns_discovered.description: Decimal(10, 2) for all monetary fields (prices, amounts)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents floating-point arithmetic errors in financial calculations

patterns_discovered.description: Auto-generated createdAt with @default(now()) and updatedAt with @updatedAt
patterns_discovered.benefit: Automatic audit trail without application code

gotchas_discovered.description: Output file (prisma/schema.prisma) is auto-generated and should not be manually edited
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear header comment warns against direct editing; developers must modify source .prisma files instead

gotchas_discovered.description: merge-schema.ts skips files starting with '.' or '_' to avoid accidentally including backup files
organizationId field marked @unique in Subscription model
gotchas_discovered.implication: Each organization can have exactly one active subscription at a time

gotchas_discovered.issue: Trial Period Optional but currentPeriodStart/End Required
gotchas_discovered.description: Trial dates (trialStart, trialEnd) are optional but current billing period dates are required
gotchas_discovered.consideration: currentPeriodStart/End must be set even during trial periods

gotchas_discovered.description: Line items stored as JSON array instead of normalized Invoice_LineItem table
gotchas_discovered.trade_off: Simpler schema vs. inability to query individual line items with SQL

deliverables_completed: Created subscription.prisma with Plan, Subscription, and Invoice models
Implemented comprehensive merge-schema.ts script with proper error handling
key_achievements: Established modular schema architecture supporting multi-file organization
Implemented intelligent file ordering to ensure datasource/generator definitions come first
Created production-ready billing domain models with proper enums and relationships
Added detailed comments and documentation in both schema and script
Implemented robust error handling and user feedback in merge script
approach_outcome.execution_notes: First attempt succeeded; no rework required. Implementation follows established patterns from DATABASE_SCHEMA.md and integrates seamlessly with existing project structure.

recommendations.recommendation: Add validation to ensure Plan IDs referenced in Subscriptions exist before deletion
recommendations.reasoning: Foreign key constraints exist but no soft-delete or archival strategy for Plans

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding auditLog entries for subscription state transitions (status changes, cancellations)
recommendations.reasoning: Subscription changes are critical for billing; detailed audit trail would be valuable

recommendations.recommendation: Add constraint to ensure currentPeriodEnd > currentPeriodStart
recommendations.reasoning: Currently no database-level enforcement; prevents invalid billing periods

recommendations.recommendation: Generate ERD (Entity Relationship Diagram) from merged schema for stakeholder visualization
recommendations.reasoning: Would help non-technical team members understand subscription model relationships

recommendations.recommendation: Create test cases for merge-schema.ts to verify correct file ordering and content concatenation
recommendations.reasoning: Script is critical infrastructure; regression could break entire schema generation

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor Invoice queries on (subscriptionId, status) and (status, dueDate) indexes for query performance
recommendations.reasoning: These indexes support common filtering patterns but should be validated under production load

recommendations.recommendation: Add npm script alias in package.json for easy schema merging and regeneration of Prisma client
recommendations.reasoning: Would streamline developer workflow for schema modifications
